data.title: Domino's Pizza Focusing on Long-Term Growth Despite Near-Term Pressures, UBS Says
data.description:
Domino's Pizza is well positioned to accelerate US same-store sales momentum in 2025 through several new initiatives and despite some near-term pressures, including modest expectations for Q4 and Q1 performance, the focus is on long-term growth, UBS Securities said in a note on Friday.

Everyday value promotions, marketing and promotional events such as the $9.99 Any Crust Any Toppings promotion, menu innovations like potential stuffed crust pizza and Mac and Cheese offering, and contributions from third-party delivery platforms could strengthen Domino's same-store sales and increase market share in 2025, the firm added.

Even though near-term trends are expected to be under pressure from industry headwinds and unfavorable comparisons, these initiatives are anticipated to lead to a notable acceleration in sales, according to the note.

"We believe global store development should accelerate over the coming years as major international closures largely enter the rearview, with solid new open demand in the US and key international markets such as India and China, and a recovery from the Middle East conflict impact," UBS said.

UBS has a buy rating on Domino's stock with a $540 price target.

data.title: Best Crypto for 100x Gains as Top Analyst Suggests Crypto Hype Is Not Over
data.description:
Jamie Coutts, the chief digital asset analyst of Real Vision, says that the current crypto cycle is far from over, considering global liquidity conditions. Coutts drew a comparison between the global money supply (M2) and the crypto market. Of course, crypto movements depend a lot on liquidity. The more money there is in the markets, the more it tends to go up. Another side of the coin is adoption. As more people use crypto, it becomes more valuable and sees a price increase. Crypto benefits from both liquidity and increasing structural growth.

Coutts points out that there has been an increase in global liquidity pushed by the weakening dollar. As the currency becomes weak, more people tend to put their money in crypto and stocks. Current global liquidity may soon cross last year’s highs as well, which will indicate a new regime in the crypto industry.

More Countries May Buy $BTC

Jamie also pointed out that many developing countries have been adding Bitcoin to their sovereign funds for over a year now. This goes to show the confidence of large institutions in the token. As crypto adoption increases, more countries are expected to aggressively add Bitcoin.

El Salvador can be a good example of this. The country has been quietly accumulating Bitcoins and now holds over 6,000 $BTC. During 2025, El Salvador bought an average of 1 $BTC a day — despite increasing IMF pressure and a $1.4B agreement, which requires the government to make Bitcoin voluntary for businesses.

Clearly, the trend is slowly shifting towards a pro-crypto side, backed by solid liquidity data as well as institutional support. If you don’t want to miss out on the bull run, here are the best cryptos you can invest in during their presale stages for massive potential gains.

1. BTC Bull Token ($BTCBULL) – Best Crypto to Maximize Gains from Bitcoin’s Rally

Just when you thought meme coins could no longer surprise you, something generational like the BTC Bull Token ($BTCBULL) crops up. We say ‘generational’ because it’s the first-ever (and currently the ONLY) crypto to offer free $BTC to token holders.

To qualify for $BTC airdrops, all you have to do is purchase $BTCBULL tokens and store them in Best Wallet. Then, every time Bitcoin reaches a new landmark figure ($150K, $200K, $250K, etc.) for the very first time, you’ll receive $BTC as your reward for loyalty. Even more interesting is how the project plans to titillate the market post-launch. It’ll keep burning a part of its total token supply to boost demand and ensure the token’s price keeps climbing. These ‘burn events’ will occur every time $BTC’s price increases by $25K.

You can grab 1 $BTCBULL for just $0.002375 if you get in right now. It’s worth pointing out that BTC Bull Token has become one of the best crypto presales of 2025. It has raised over $2.5M in just a week, after all.

2. Solaxy ($SOLX) – Solana Layer 2 Token Solving Scalability Issues

Solaxy ($SOLX) isn’t your usual meme coin. It combines classic meme appeal with real-world application, which makes it one of the best cryptos to invest in right now.

The project’s developers looked at how well Ethereum’s Layer 2 chains have been doing and decided to create the first-ever Layer 2 solution for Solana, which, by the way, is in dire need of it. Solaxy will revolutionize Solana by alleviating its pain points. Processing transactions in batches and outside the main network will make Solana faster and cheaper than it already is.

Considering Solaxy could play a massive role in stirring up the next Solana wave, it could also be the biggest beneficiary from the same. The $SOLX presale has been doing very well, in fact, and has raised a whopping $22.8M at the time of writing.

You can join the gravy train by shelling out just $0.001642 per $SOLX. For more info, here’s a guide on how to buy $SOLX.

3. Meme Index ($MEMEX) – A Fresh Approach to Investing in Meme Coins

Meme coins are known for their volatility. +100% gains in a day? Yes, please! However, volatility goes both ways, and there’s no denying the risk meme coins carry. The same volatility is also keeping a lot of investors from entering the space, especially those accustomed to investing in safe stock market instruments such as index funds.

Well, what if there were a similar trading instrument for meme coins? Very soon, there will be. All hail Meme Index ($MEMEX).

Meme Index offers four different baskets of meme coins, each with a varying degree of risk, reward, and volatility. By investing in several meme coins at once and choosing a basket that suits your investment style, you’ll be able to create an ideal crypto portfolio for yourself.

The Meme Titan Index, for instance, only contains well-established ‘safe’ meme coins like $DOGE. Great for risk-averse investors. Then there’s the Meme Frenzy Index, which contains cryptos that can explode. Riskier but also exponentially more rewarding.

4. SUNDOG ($SUNDOG) – TRON Dog Meme Coin Breaking Records

data.title: Sika Expects Difficult Year for European Auto Business, CEO Says — Market Talk
data.description: Sika expects a difficult year for its automotive business in Europe, CEO Thomas Hasler says in an interview. The Switzerland-based specialty-chemical company is running at the output level for cars in Europe, which is far below the usual going rate, indicating a huge backlog, Hasler says. "Consumers are hesitant to invest. The electrification path is somehow stalled and incentives are not in place to stimulate," he says. However, the fleet in Europe is aging drastically and will have to recover in the years to come, Hasler says. This year is probably still going to see declining volumes, also given the geopolitical uncertainty, he adds. Shares trade 0.5% higher at 236.70 Swiss francs. (nina.kienle@wsj.com)

data.title: Crypto exchange Bybit hacked as over $1 billion worth of ETH leaves wallets
data.description:
Bybit, the Singapore-based centralized crypto exchange, appears to have been hacked. Early estimates suggest the exchange has lost over $1 billion worth of ETH, though the investigation is ongoing.

"Bybit ETH multisig cold wallet just made a transfer to our warm wallet about 1 hr ago. It appears that this specific transaction was musked, all the signers saw the musked UI which showed the correct address and the URL was from Safe. However the signing message was to change the smart contract logic of our ETH cold wallet," Bybit cO-founder and CEO Ben Zhou posted to X. "This resulted Hacker took control of the specific ETH cold wallet we signed and transferred all ETH in the cold wallet to this unidentified address. Please rest assured that all other cold wallets are secure. All withdraws are NORMAL."

On Tuesday, the exchange announced it would be performing scheduled maintenance on its live server today stretching into tomorrow. 

According to onchain data, the attacker appears to have gained access to ByBit’s hot wallets and has been using it to swap holdings of stETH for ETH and redeem mETH. 

This story is breaking and will be updated as The Block learns more.

Disclaimer: The Block is an independent media outlet that delivers news, research, and data. As of November 2023, Foresight Ventures is a majority investor of The Block. Foresight Ventures invests in other companies in the crypto space. Crypto exchange Bitget is an anchor LP for Foresight Ventures. The Block continues to operate independently to deliver objective, impactful, and timely information about the crypto industry. Here are our current financial disclosures.

data.title: Grab 2025 Growth Momentum Stays Strong, Morgan Stanley Says
data.description:
Grab could see accelerating top-line growth in 2025, driven by growing penetration and company-specific initiatives to expand its total addressable market, Morgan Stanley said in a Friday note.

The firm expects deliveries' gross merchandise value to grow by 14% and mobility GMV to rise 19%, reinforcing strong growth momentum. Deliveries' adjusted EBITDA margin is expected to expand to 2.1% from 1.7% last year, while Mobility margins are expected to remain largely stable despite reinvestments in select markets.

The firm anticipates adjusted Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization to rise 60% year over year, surpassing management's guidance and consensus estimates, according to the note.

For Financial Services, Morgan Stanley anticipates losses to narrow to $80 million from $105 million, as Grab expands its digital bank loan books in Singapore and Malaysia.

The analysts noted that the company is in a prime position to drive the adoption of autonomous vehicles, given its leading utilization rates. The company said it is in discussions with regulators and potential partners regarding the technology, according to the note.

The firm also highlighted Grab's strong growth in Indonesia and its strategic advantage in cross-selling across its ecosystem.

Morgan Stanley reiterated the company's rating of overweight and increased the price target to $5.70 from $5.50.

data.title: AlphaValue/Baader Europe Ups Schneider Electric Rating, Price Target Amid Positive Outlook
data.description:
AlphaValue/Baader Europe revised its opinion and target price on Schneider Electric following the company's strong 2024 results and positive 2025 outlook.

The research firm on Friday changed the French energy management and automation company's rating to reduce from sell and raised the target price to 265 euros from 215 euros.

"We have raised our target price by c.23%, led by a c.40% increase in our NAV and a c.25% increase in our DCF valuation. In addition, we have also raised the premium vs. peers to 30% from 25% owing to the higher exposure to the high-growth Datacentre market," analysts said.

The company is also anticipated to benefit from solid demand tailwinds and high structural growth, particularly in its energy management business.

data.title: Abbott Declares 405th Consecutive Quarterly Dividend
data.description:
Abbott Declares 405th Consecutive Quarterly Dividend

PR Newswire

ABBOTT PARK, Ill., Feb. 21, 2025

ABBOTT PARK, Ill., Feb. 21, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — The board of directors of Abbott today declared a quarterly common dividend of 59 cents per share.

This marks the 405(th) consecutive quarterly dividend to be paid by Abbott since 1924. The cash dividend is payable May 15, 2025, to shareholders of record at the close of business on April 15, 2025.

Abbott has increased its dividend payout for 53 consecutive years and is a member of the S&P 500 Dividend Aristocrats Index, which tracks companies that have increased dividends annually for at least 25 consecutive years.

About Abbott:

Abbott is a global healthcare leader that helps people live more fully at all stages of life. Our portfolio of life-changing technologies spans the spectrum of healthcare, with leading businesses and products in diagnostics, medical devices, nutritionals and branded generic medicines. Our 114,000 colleagues serve people in more than 160 countries.

Connect with us at www.abbott.com and on LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram, X and YouTube.

View original content:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/abbott-declares-405th-consecutive-quarterly-dividend-302382388.html

SOURCE Abbott

/CONTACT: Abbott Media: Kate Dyer, (224) 668-9965; Abbott Financial: Michael Comilla, (224) 668-1872

data.title: Amazon Just Did the Unthinkable--Walmart's Reign as Retail King Is Under Threat
data.description:
Amazon just pulled off something it's never done beforeovertaking Walmart in quarterly revenue. In its latest earnings report, Amazon posted $187.8 billion in Q4 sales, outpacing Walmart's $180.6 billion. This marks the first time the e-commerce titan has edged out the retail giant on a quarterly basis, shaking up a competitive landscape that has long been dominated by Walmart. However, Amazon's Q4 includes a bigger chunk of the holiday shopping season, giving it an edge in seasonal sales. Despite this, the gap between these two retail heavyweights is narrowing, and investors are watching closely.

That said, Walmart isn't backing down. It's still expected to retain its title as the top revenue generator for the full year, with projected annual sales of $708.7 billion compared to Amazon's estimated $700.8 billion. But here's the twistWalmart is leaning harder into e-commerce, a market Amazon has owned for decades. Meanwhile, Amazon has something Walmart doesn't: AWS, its cloud computing juggernaut, which continues to fuel its profitability. As these two giants refine their strategies, the fight for retail supremacy is no longer just about who sells moreit's about who adapts faster.

For investors, this shift is more than just a headline. It signals a changing dynamic in consumer spending, digital commerce, and corporate strategy. Walmart's physical store network is still a major advantage, but Amazon's data-driven, logistics-heavy model is proving its strength at scale. If Amazon can keep this momentum going, this quarter's win might not just be a flukeit could be the start of a long-term shake-up in the retail hierarchy.

data.title: This Under-the-Radar AI Stock Has Jumped 210% in 2025. What Comes Next. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Adam Clark

Looking for the hottest play on the artificial-intelligence boom this year? VNET Group might not be a household name, but its U.S.-listed stock has more than tripled in value already this year.

VNET is a Chinese data-center operator. Investors are betting it will benefit from a spending spree on cloud-computing infrastructure from the likes of TikTok-owner ByteDance and e-commerce company Alibaba.

Those hopes were given another boost on Thursday, when Alibaba said it expects its investment in cloud computing and AI infrastructure in the next three years will exceed that of the past decade. Analysts at Jefferies put that at more than $40 billion over the period.

VNET's American depositary receipts , or ADRs, were up 8.3% at $15.90 in early trading Friday, adding to a more than 200% gain since the start of the year. Shares of peer GDS Holdings was climbing 4.1%, having more than doubled in 2025 so far.

"The most straightforward trickle-down positive [from Alibaba] is data centers. VNET risk-reward is attractive here. Despite the rally, it's the cheapest around," wrote Jefferies equity sales specialist William Beavington.

VNET doesn't get a lot of Wall Street attention, but Jefferies analysts have been fans for a while. The company was featured on the investment bank's global list of top picks in January, citing a wave of infrastructure demand from Chinese cloud-computing companies.

At this point it's worth some words of caution. Chinese tech stocks have rocketed before, only to crash in the wake of a regulatory crackdown. VNET's ADRs traded as high as $41 in mid-2021, before collapsing to below $2 apiece early last year. VNET's latest rally has already taken its shares past the Jefferies target price of $10.73.

There's no guarantee that Beijing's current benevolent attitude to tech champions such as ByteDance, Alibaba Group Holding, and Tencent will continue, however, and VNET's fortunes are closely tied to its customers. At the moment those companies can buy hardware such as Nvidia's H20 AI chips to install in VNET's data centers, but that could change in future under U.S. or Chinese policy.

VNET has also had a volatile ownership situation. Its founder Josh Sheng Chen last year withdrew an outstanding offer he had made in September 2022 to take the company private. That came after government-owned Shandong Hi-Speed Holdings Group acquired $299 million worth of new shares to become the company's largest shareholder.

As is the case with dozens of Chinese entities listed on U.S. exchanges, foreign investors who buy ADRs in VNET are buying shares in a shell company based in the Cayman Islands, to which the operating entity pledges its cash flows and profits.

Write to Adam Clark at adam.clark@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Fossil-Fuel Companies Are Lobbying Trump to Save a Green-Fuel Program — WSJ
data.description:
By Yusuf Khan

Oil-and-gas companies and renewable-energy groups are teaming up to save a Biden-era tax incentive to produce green hydrogen, amid worries the Trump administration will remove the concession as part of a push to ax green subsidies.

In a letter with more than 100 signatories, industry groups representing oil-and-gas companies, renewable power companies and chemicals firms pushed the House, Senate and Trump administration to keep the 45V tax credit, which is dedicated to creating hydrogen as a fuel source in the U.S.

"The hydrogen market is growing at a rapid pace internationally and the U.S. has a unique opportunity to solidify and advance its leadership in this global commodity," the Feb. 18 letter said. "To ensure our country retains its leading position, we need continued policy support, particularly as other countries are investing billions in hydrogen." The letter was addressed to House Speaker Mike Johnson, Senate Majority Leader John Thune, Senate Finance Committee Chairman Mike Crapo and House Ways and Means Chairman Jason Smith.

Signatories included aircraft manufacturer Airbus, chemicals giant Dow and automaker General Motors, while industry groups including the American Petroleum Institute, which represents companies like Exxon Mobil and Chevron, also signed the letter.

The tax credit, which was finalized by the Biden administration just before President Trump taking office, allows for up to $3 per kilogram of hydrogen produced. With hydrogen a nascent industry in the U.S., the credit has been seen as an enabling force for production, both in pushing energy producers to start making the fuel and for heavy industries like the steel sector to use it as a fuel source.

Since Trump started his second term, worries have mounted that the Inflation Reduction Act would be dismantled by his administration. Many IRA subsidies have flowed to Republican states, meaning if the Act were repealed, much of those funds would be pulled.

However, support for the hydrogen credits has come from both sides of the political aisle, with California Gov. Gavin Newsom, a Democrat, and Texas Gov. Greg Abbott, a Republican, prominent supporters.

The credit in its current form works in a tiered system, with the maximum $3 for projects dedicated to producing so called green hydrogen. That is hydrogen produced using renewable sources of energy, making it a largely emission-free fuel source. Lower tiers allow for production of hydrogen using fossil fuels such as natural gas, but where the carbon is captured, known as blue hydrogen.

"It is a linchpin for enabling the entire spectrum of hydrogen in the United States," said Frank Wolak, CEO of the Fuel Cell and Hydrogen Energy Association, an industry group. Wolak said hydrogen chimes with the Trump administration's aim of energy dominance, and keeping the credit would allow the U.S. to become the key producer of hydrogen and ammonia globally. "We do not want to see that investment thwarted," Wolak added.

As part of the push to keep the tax credit, fossil-fuel firms have been pushing to loosen the tier system to allow for the use of more fossil fuels such as natural gas to be considered for tax credits. In particular, using blended natural gas has been suggested. That entails renewable natural gas produced from food waste and animal manure and traditional forms being mixed together to help produce the hydrogen. The carbon would then be captured to create blue hydrogen.

Last year 16.4 million metric tons of hydrogen were produced in the U.S., according to the Business Council for Sustainable Energy, an advocacy group. However, in a report released this week, the BCSE, with BloombergNEF, said current production levels had been inhibited by political uncertainty around tax credit guidelines finalized only in January. It added that 77% of the U.S.'s low carbon hydrogen production that has been announced is blue hydrogen, which is less expensive to produce.

"Expanding hydrogen production will enhance America's energy security, drive economic growth, and create jobs," said Amy Chiang, chief sustainability and external affairs officer at Topsoe, a Danish energy transition technologies firm which also signed the letter. Chiang added that Topsoe sees the tax credit as "essential for enabling the private sector to make long-term investments that will strengthen the nation's hydrogen sector."

Write to Yusuf Khan at yusuf.khan@wsj.com

data.title: Cenovus Energy Downgraded at National Bank; Target Cut at Both RBC and National
data.description:
Cenovus Energy's stock rating and price target were downgraded at National Bank while the target was cut at RBC Capital Markets after the company reported results for the fourth quarter of 2024.

RBC Capital Markets said Thursday that it cut Cenovus' price target to $25 from $26 while maintaining its Outperform rating.

National Bank of Canada on Thursday also reduced Cenovus' price target to C$25 from $28 while its stock rating was downgraded to Sector Perform from Outperform.

For its part, RBC noted Cenovus delivered mixed fourth-quarter performance amid 1% better

upstream production of 816,000 boe/d and bottom-line results that were in-line with the bank's estimates, but shy of market expectations.

"Our constructive stance towards Cenovus reflects its upstream operating momentum and growth, strong balance sheet, solid leadership team and shareholder returns policy," RBC said. "But its soft quarterly results continue to weigh heavily on market sentiment. Like a fine wine, Cenovus' outlook should improve with time, but sustained improvement in its US refinery segment remains a key ingredient supporting relative outperformance."

Meanwhile, National Bank said its revised target price was based on a 5.0x 2025e EV/DACF multiple (down from 5.5x), which is a discount to its peers and reflects the lower margin downstream segment.

National added it has "growing concerns" around the company's culture now that as many as four to five members of senior management have departed over the last six months.

"As margin capture improves and the management transition and integration evolves with no cultural or operational disruptions, we see meaningful upside potential to today's equity value," the bank said.

Cenovus' share price was down 4% at last look on the TSX, amid broader market losses.

data.title: Innodata Shares Hit 52-Week High After 4Q Earnings Beat
data.description:
By Chris Wack

Innodata shares were up 28% to $69.93 after the company reported fourth-quarter earnings that beat analysts' expectations.

The stock hit its 52-week high of $71 a share earlier in the session, and is up 716% in the past 12 months.

The data-engineering company late Thursday said it had quarterly earnings of $10.3 million, or 31 cents a share, compared with earnings of $1.7 million, or 5 cents a share, in the same quarter a year earlier. Analysts polled by FactSet were looking for earnings of $7.2 million, or 21 cents a share.

Revenue for the quarter was $59.2 million, compared with the prior year's $26.1 million and the $53 million analysts were expecting.

Cash, cash equivalents and short-term investments were $46.9 million as of Dec. 31, compared with $13.8 million as of Dec. 31, 2023.

For 2025, the company is forecasting 40% or more revenue growth, based primarily on won deals and near-in, forecastable business.

Write to Chris Wack at chris.wack@wsj.com

Several top executives at Clearwater Analytics Holdings have recently sold shares of the company's Class A Common Stock to satisfy tax withholding obligations related to the vesting of performance stock units.

James S. Cox, the Chief Financial Officer, sold 101,808 shares at a price of $30.0707 per share, totaling $3,061,437. Following the transaction, Cox directly owns 307,854 shares of the company.

Souvik Das, the Chief Technology Officer, sold 76,844 shares at the same price, totaling $2,310,752. Post-sale, Das directly owns 68,902 shares.

Sethi Subi, the Chief Client Officer, disposed of 62,482 shares, also at $30.0707 each, for a total of $1,878,877. Subi's direct ownership stands at 144,973 shares after the sale.

Sahai Sandeep, the CEO, sold 218,687 shares at the price of $30.0707 per share, amounting to $6,576,071. Sandeep now directly owns 681,588 shares.

Erickson Scott Stanley, the Chief Revenue Officer, sold 76,256 shares, totaling $2,293,071. Following this transaction, Stanley directly owns 78,363 shares.

data.title: These Stocks Are Moving the Most Today: UnitedHealth, Alibaba, Rivian, Celsius, Booking, Block, Akamai, Dropbox, and More — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Joe Woelfel

Stocks fell Friday, a day after Wall Street tumbled following a warning from Walmart that the world's largest retailer expected a slower fiscal year.

These stocks were moving Friday:

UnitedHealth declined 9% after The Wall Street Journal reported the Justice Department was investigating the healthcare giant's Medicare billing practices in recent months. The report, which cited people familiar with the matter, said the DOJ probe aims to examine the healthcare company's practices for documenting diagnoses that trigger extra payments to its Medicare Advantage plans. Other healthcare stocks fell — Humana declined 4%, and CVS Health was down 1.6%.

Walmart was down 0.9%. The stock declined 6.5% on Thursday after the company issued weak guidance for fiscal 2026, citing "uncertainties" in the economy and consumer behavior. The retailer said it expects net sales to increase by 3% to 4% versus analysts' expectations of around 4% growth.

U.S.-listed shares of Alibaba rose 4.9%. The Chinese e-commerce giant closed with a gain of 8.1% on Thursday after quarterly earnings beat Wall Street expectations, and after The Wall Street Journal reported late in the session that GameStop CEO Ryan Cohen in recent months has boosted his stake in Alibaba to $1 billion, or about 7 million shares. The Journal report cited people familiar with the matter.

Rivian Automotive, the electric-vehicle company, posted fourth-quarter gross profit of $170 million, higher than analysts' estimates of $64 million. Sales were $1.7 billion versus forecasts of $1.5 billion. The stock, however, was down 5% after Rivian said it expects an adjusted loss before interest and taxes of between $1.7 billion and $1.9 billion in 2025, below consensus that called for a loss around $1.69 billion. The company also said it expects to deliver 46,000 to 51,000 vehicles in 2025, with the midpoint of that range of 48,500 below Wall Street projections of about 55,000 vehicles.

Tesla, the largest U.S. EV maker, was down 0.6% after declining 1.7% on Thursday. Lucid Group fell 0.9%.

Online travel company Booking Holdings reported fourth-quarter earnings and bookings that beat analysts' expectations. Room nights booked rose 13% to 261 million in the period, better than analysts' expectations of 250 million, while gross bookings gained 17%. The stock was rising 1.4%.

Celsius Holdings jumped 26% after the maker of energy drinks posted fourth-quarter earnings and revenue that topped Wall Street expectations and announced an agreement to buy rival Alani Nu, a growing energy-drink brand fueled by social-media influencers, for $1.8 billion in cash and stock.

Block, the parent company of Square and Cash App, was falling 12% after the fintech company reported fourth-quarter adjusted earnings of 71 cents a share on revenue of $6.03 billion, missing analysts' expectations for earnings of 88 cents on revenue of $6.3 billion. Gross profit in the quarter was $2.31 billion, up 14% from a year earlier. Block said it expects gross profit growth in 2025 to increase 15% to $10.22 billion, below Wall Street estimates of $10.28 billion.

Akamai Technologies was down 13% after the cloud computing company said it expects first-quarter adjusted earnings of between $1.54 and $1.59 a share on revenue of $1 billion to $1.02 billion. Analysts had been expecting earnings of $1.65 a share on revenue of $1.05 billion.

Shares of Palantir Technologies were down 0.9%. The software company declined 5.2% on Thursday, extending losses from Wednesday when it lost 10%. The decline followed reports of potential U.S. defense budget cuts, and came after CEO Alex Karp adopted a new trading plan to sell up to 9,975,000 Palantir shares.

Coinbase Global rose 2.9% after the crypto exchange said staff at the Securities and Exchange Commission will recommend dismissal of a two-year-old lawsuit that aimed to regulate the company as a stock exchange.

Dropbox fell 11%. The cloud storage company reported fourth-quarter adjusted earnings and revenue that beat Wall Street estimates but said during its conference call that it expects paying users in 2025 to decline by roughly 1.5% to 300,000.

Five9 rose 3.6% after the call-center software company posted fourth-quarter earnings of 79 cents a share on revenue of $278.7 million, beating analysts' estimates for profit of 70 cents on revenue of $267.8 million.

Write to Joe Woelfel at joseph.woelfel@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Baltic Dry Index Rally Continues
data.description:
The Baltic Exchange's dry bulk sea freight index, which measures shipping rates for vessels transporting dry bulk commodities, advanced for the seventh session on Friday, rising 40 points to a fresh one-month high of 981 points.

The capesize index, which typically tracks vessels transporting 150,000-ton cargoes such as iron ore and coal, surged 74 points to a more than three-week high of 991 points; and the panamax index, which usually tracks vessels carrying 60,000-70,000 tons of coal or grain, increased 26 points to a more than three-month high of 1,170 points.

Among smaller vessels, the supramax index advanced 20 points to 886 points, extending gains for the 14th consecutive day.

data.description: Five9 has an average rating of overweight and mean price target of $54.65, according to analysts polled by FactSet.
